title: Leptorkis forbesii
intro: Leptorkis forbesii, also known as the "Indian Plum," is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Leptorkis forbesii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Leptorkis forbesii is often prepared into various herbal formulations, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, skin diseases, and respiratory infections.

constituents: Leptorkis forbesii, also known as the yellow ginger, contains several active constituents that are responsible for its medicinal properties.
